_comment: Edit `bindings` only — friendly control name -> action. Actions: tab_next, tab_prev, workspace_next, workspace_prev, pane_left, pane_right, pane_up, pane_down, pane_zoom, scroll_up, scroll_down, enter, escape, voice, dictation, noop. Scrolling is analog (right stick) configured under settings.scroll. The `profile` maps each friendly name to the raw SDL index; run `cc-controller calibrate` to rebuild it. Edit interactively with `cc-controller config bind` / `config edit`, or `config set <path> <value>`.
_backend_comment: Which multiplexer the same bindings drive: "herdr" (default) or "tmux". Actions are abstract — tmux reads tab=window, workspace=session, pane=pane. So tab_next/prev (ZL/ZR) become next/previous-window, workspace_next/prev (L/R) switch sessions, pane_* select-pane. Override per-run with `--backend <name>`; for remote mode the backend is chosen here (the controller sends backend-neutral intents and forwards the resolved backend to `cc-controller host`).

remote._comment: Used by `cc-controller remote`: where the multiplexer machine is. ssh_host is any valid ssh target (user@host or a ~/.ssh/config alias). host_bin is the cc-controller binary on that machine (PATH name or absolute path); `remote` runs `<host_bin> host` over ssh and streams intents to it. Leave ssh_host empty to require --host. Ignored by `local`.

profile._comment: friendly name -> raw SDL index. Defaults are for the Switch Pro via SDL; if buttons feel wrong, run: cc-controller calibrate
